title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host process ID namespace

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host process ID namespace
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostPID flag set to true.
input_row.rationale: A container running in the host's PID namespace can inspect processes running outside the container. If the container also has access to ptrace capabilities this can be used to escalate privileges outside of the container. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers to share the host PID namespace. If you need to run containers which require hostPID, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods defined with spec.hostPID: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.audit: Fetch hostPID from each pod with get pods -A -o=jsonpath=$'{range .items[*]}{@.metadata.name}: {@.spec.hostPID}\n{end}'
input_row.remediation: Configure the Admission Controller to restrict the admission of hostPID containers. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of hostPID containers. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/
input_row.impact: Pods defined with spec.hostPID: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be evaluated using Kubernetes APIs and kubectl commands to check for the presence of pods with the `spec.hostPID` set to true.
- The audit command provided in the control description directly utilizes `kubectl`, which confirms that this property can be queried without human judgment.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_hostpid_restrictions

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get pods -A -o=jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{@.metadata.name}: {@.spec.hostPID}{"\n"}{end}'` to list all pods across all namespaces and their `hostPID` settings.
- The expected output should show `false` for all pods to comply with the control. If any pod shows `true`, it indicates a violation of the control.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: All pods have `spec.hostPID` set to `false`.
  - Fail: Any pod has `spec.hostPID` set to `true`.
